channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
[An RX Global Event] Infosecurity Europe
Infosecurity Europe: UK Firms Prioritize AI Threat Preparedness as Cyber Risks Evolve
item.description: UK organizations are prioritizing AI-driven cybersecurity as 43% cite AI-powered attacks as their top risk, prompting significant investment in advanced threat defense

item.title: Attackers Hijack Red Hat npm Scope to Steal Cloud Secrets
item.description: Attackers backdoored 32 packages in Red Hat's official npm scope to steal cloud and CI secrets

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
item.description: <p>Hackers published 96 malicious package versions, injected with a credential-stealing worm similar to Mini Shai-Hulud.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/supply-chain-attack-hits-32-red-hat-npm-packages/">Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Pakistan-Linked SideCopy Targets Afghanistan Finance Ministry with Xeno RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a spear-phishing campaign likely undertaken by the Pakistan-aligned SideCopy group targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance with an open-source remote access trojan called Xeno RAT.
"The campaign opens with a spear phishing delivery - a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file bearing a carefully crafted Pashto-language filename,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Business Leaders Lack Understanding of Threat Intelligence, Study Warns
item.description: A new Silobreaker and SANS Institute paper examines the ‘Intelligence-Stakeholder Gap’ and what organizations must do to achieve business buy-in on threat intelligence

item.title: Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads
item.description: <p>Dashlane’s security systems automatically locked accounts to protect them against the hacking attempts.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/dashlane-brute-force-attack-leads-to-limited-encrypted-vault-downloads/">Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Oracle’s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Oracle’s monthly Critical Security Patch Update (CSPU) rollouts are meant to deliver critical fixes faster.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/oracles-first-monthly-patches-resolve-77-vulnerabilities/">Oracle&#8217;s First Monthly Patches Resolve 77 Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
item.description:
Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party.
On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Surviving a LockBit Ransomware Attack: The ROI of Visibility | UpGuard
item.description: Read how veteran CISO Nick Gicinto saved his alma mater from a LockBit ransomware attack by discarding the standard playbook.

item.title: 12 Best Third-Party Risk Management Software Solutions (2026) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn which contenders are the top TPRM solution options in the market. Compare their features to make the best decision based on your requirements.

item.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
item.description: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of &quot;strong bilateral cooperation&quot; between the European Commission and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
item.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
item.description: <p>The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/wp-maps-pro-vulnerability-exploited-to-take-over-wordpress-sites/">WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet
item.description: <p>Dutch authorities seized command-and-control servers tied to a botnet of infected computers, smartphones, and tablets that was allegedly used to power a residential proxy network and facilitate cybercrime.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/dutch-police-dismantle-massive-17-million-device-botnet/">Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
item.description:
A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm.
"This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and potential
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Vulnerability Disclosure in the Age of AI
item.description: <p>New article: &#8220;<a href="https://cyberdefensereview.army.mil/Portals/6/Documents/2026-vol11-iss2/CDR_V11_N2_Hathaway.pdf">Responsible Disclosure in the Age of AI: A Call for Urgent Action</a>,&#8221; by Melissa Hathaway.</p> <blockquote><p><b>Abstract:</b> Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping the balance between vulnerability discovery and remediation. Frontier AI models are now capable of autonomously identifying exploitable software vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed and scale. This development exposes decades of accumulated technical debt created by a software industry that prioritized rapid deployment over secure-by-design engineering practices. Drawing on the evolution of software assurance, vulnerability disclosure frameworks, and U.S. cyber policy, this perspective argues that the current moment represents a strategic inflection point for governments, industry, and critical infrastructure operators. The author examines the growing tension between offensive and defensive equities in cyberspace, the emergence of AI-enabled vulnerability discovery capabilities in both the U.S. and China, and the increasing risks posed by unsupported legacy systems and AI-assisted code generation practices. Responsible disclosure can no longer remain a reactive or fragmented process, but must become a coordinated national and international resilience effort involving governments, software vendors, infrastructure operators, and emergency response organizations. The article concludes with an urgent call for accelerated remediation, large-scale patch management coordination, and sustained investment in automated vulnerability repair capabilities before adversaries exploit this rapidly narrowing window of opportunity...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Google Chrome’s New Feature Takes Aim at Cookie Theft, Account Hijacking
item.description: <p>Chrome’s DBSC update binds login sessions to user devices, making stolen session cookies harder to reuse in account hijacking attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-chrome-dbsc-session-cookie-theft/">Google Chrome’s New Feature Takes Aim at Cookie Theft, Account Hijacking</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Microsoft Fixed Defender… But Systems Are Still Exposed
item.description: <p>Subscribe to the Cybersecurity Insider Newsletter Strengthen your organization's IT security defenses by keeping abreast of the latest cybersecurity news, solutions, and best practices. Delivered every Monday, Tuesday and Thursday Email Address By signing up to receive our newsletter, you agree to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe at any time. ...</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/videos/microsoft-fixed-defender-but-systems-are-still-exposed/">Microsoft Fixed Defender… But Systems Are Still Exposed</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Caleb Kinchlow

item.title: Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs
item.description: <p>Organizations are advised to patch CVE-2026-41089 as soon as possible, given its severity, the potential ongoing exploitation.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-windows-netlogon-vulnerability-in-attackers-crosshairs/">Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
item.description: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Critical Flowise Flaw Gives Attackers Full Server Control
item.description: Obsidian publishes PoC for a 1-click Flowise RCE that can fully compromise self-hosted servers

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
item.description:
Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues.
A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering the bar for people who already thought 'curl | sh' had a personality.
The vibe is simple: old
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Tabletop Exercise to Test How CISOs Respond to Major Supermarket Cyber-Attack
item.description: Semperis is set to bring ‘Enter the War Room: A Tabletop Experience’ to Infosecurity Europe to help cybersecurity leaders prepare to face real incidents

item.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
item.description: <p>Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/dragos-acquires-xiot-security-firm-phosphorus/">Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
item.description:
A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent.
According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial services sectors. The activity entails distributing spear-phishing emails containing ZIP attachments
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
item.description: <p>AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/as-the-pentagon-pushes-for-battlefield-ai-some-military-leaders-urge-caution/">As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: AI SOCs Will Still Need SOC Analysts, Security Vendors Say
item.description: Top cybersecurity vendors said AI won't replace entry-level – only routine ticket-taking and triage

item.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
item.description:
Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor.
A Security Growth Platform is the more precise name for what MSPs and MSSPs need from the software
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
item.description: <p>Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/19-year-old-linux-kernel-vulnerability-exposes-systems-to-root-access/">19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: FSB Group Gamaredon Hides Worm in Windows Data Streams
item.description: FSB-linked Gamaredon concealed a fileless worm in NTFS data streams to spy on Ukraine targets

item.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
item.description: <p>Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/recent-palo-alto-networks-vulnerability-exploited-for-weeks/">Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI.
The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads. The package is still available for download from the repository.
What
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Abuse Shared Content for ChatGPT Phishing Campaign
item.description: Push Security says threat actors are delivering malware hosted on chatgpt.com/s/ domain

item.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
item.description:
Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites.
WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps and OpenStreetMap with markers, listings, and advanced location features on WordPress sites. It is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Palo Alto Warns High-Severity Bug Is Being Actively Exploited
item.description: A vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS software is being exploited in attacks

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: OWASP Forms New Agentic Research Council
item.description: OWASP’s new Agentic Research Council will aim to connect academic work to operational realities on agentic AI security

item.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
item.description:
Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks.
The bot network, per the Dutch Politie and the National Cyber Security Center (NCSC), consisted of at least 17 million infected devices. More than 200 servers located in the Netherlands acted as the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that could be exploited by bad actors to set up VPN connections.
"Authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Another Squid
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.
item.dc:creator: John Klossner

item.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks.
The technique has been codenamed ChatGPhish by Permiso Security.
"The chatgpt.com response renderer trusts Markdown links and Markdown
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Dexcom Warns Stolen G7 Glucose Sensors May Pose Infection, Reading Risks
item.description: <p>Dexcom says stolen G7 sensors from two scrapped lots were sold through unauthorized channels, creating infection and reading-failure risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dexcom-g7-stolen-sensors-resold/">Dexcom Warns Stolen G7 Glucose Sensors May Pose Infection, Reading Risks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
item.description: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation-states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Carnival Data Breach Exposes Data of Nearly 6 Million Customers
item.description: <p>Carnival says a data breach exposed personal information of nearly 6 million customers after a social engineering attack tied to a single employee account.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-carnival-data-breach-6-million-customers/">Carnival Data Breach Exposes Data of Nearly 6 Million Customers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible Marimo network using a recently disclosed vulnerability.
"The attacker compromised an internet-reachable Marimo notebook via CVE-2026-39987, extracted two cloud credentials from the compromised
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
item.description: The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a variety of factors, but that could be changing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
item.description: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses In-Person IT Impersonation to Breach Systems
item.description: Threat actors from the Silent Ransom Group, aka Luna Moth, are escalating attacks by impersonating IT staff in phone calls and even showing up in person to gain direct access to victim systems

item.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence &amp; Sexploitation
item.description: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: New Russia-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025.
GREYVIBE, per WithSecure, is assessed to be a Russian-speaking group operating broadly in the Russian time zone, with the activities aligning with Kremlin state interests, specifically when it comes to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chilling Effects
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
item.description:
Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. It now means something bigger: employees building full applications with AI, wiring them into production systems, and publishing them on the open internet. Without Security or IT in the loop.
The artifact moved from a prompt to a product. The risk surface moved with it.
In The Shadow Builders report (get it here), a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: CyCOS Project Expands to Support UK SMEs as CIISec Takes Over
item.description: From a research-driven pilot, the Cybersecurity Communities of Support (CyCOS) is about to be handed over to CIISec

item.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon client IDs and PFX certificates.
According to Socket, versions 2.0.0 through 2.0.4 of "Sicoob.Sdk" contain functionality to exfiltrate sensitive information, including PFX certificates that are used to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese Hackers Exploit Iran War to Target Maritime and Energy Companies
item.description: ESET’s 2026 APT Activity Report suggests China-backed APTs are using instability in the region to target victims, as well as continuing activity against organizations around the globe

item.title: AI-Generated npm Malware Leaks Its Own GitHub Token
item.description: Sloppy AI-generated npm infostealer leaked its own GitHub token, exposing the operator

item.title: This month in security with Tony Anscombe – May 2026 edition
item.description: In this roundup, Tony looks at attacks against Polish water treatment facilities, how AI-directed attacks failed in Mexico, and what Google believes is the first AI-generated zero-day exploit

item.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
item.description:
The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through March and April 2026.
"Kimsuky employed a range of tailored social engineering tactics, such as spoofing security software installation pages and crafting a fake Webex meeting page that leveraged
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Nominate Now for the Global Executive Assessor Roundtable (GEAR)
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: ShinyHunters Alleges 42M Records Stolen from Charter Communications
item.description: <p>Charter confirmed a cyber incident after ShinyHunters claimed it stole Spectrum customer data through vishing and SaaS account access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-charter-shinyhunters-cyber-incident/">ShinyHunters Alleges 42M Records Stolen from Charter Communications</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
item.description: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Krispy Kreme Settlement Deadline Nears: Eligible Members Could Claim Up to $3,500
item.description: <p>Krispy Kreme data breach settlement claims are due June 22. See who qualifies, payment options, key deadlines, and what eligible people need to file.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-krispy-kreme-data-breach-settlement-claims-deadline/">Krispy Kreme Settlement Deadline Nears: Eligible Members Could Claim Up to $3,500</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
item.description:
A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions.
The security flaw, per Rapid7, is rated 9.4 on the CVSS scoring system. It does not have a CVE identifier.
"The vulnerability allows any authenticated user to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 25 Security Vulnerabilities That Have Defined the 2020s (Thus Far) | UpGuard
item.description: From Log4j to SolarWinds, discover the 25 critical security vulnerabilities that have reshaped cybersecurity thus far in the 2020s.

item.title: FBI Warns Companies About Ransom Gang’s Fake IT Support Tactics
item.description: <p>The FBI warns Silent Ransom Group is targeting US law firms with phishing, fake IT calls, and in-person visits to steal data for extortion.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-silent-ransom-group-physical-impersonation-fbi-warning/">FBI Warns Companies About Ransom Gang’s Fake IT Support Tactics</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Apple May Bring Android-Style Theft Detection to iPhones
item.description: <p>Apple is reportedly testing an iPhone anti-snatching feature that would lock stolen devices using motion signals and checks for familiar locations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-iphone-anti-snatching-lock-feature/">Apple May Bring Android-Style Theft Detection to iPhones</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: David Curry

item.title: Agentic AI Isn't Risky; the Way Orgs Deploy It Is
item.description: AI agents aren't black boxes — they're models interacting with software tools. The risk lies in their overlap.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Attackers Move Past Typosquatting to Realistic Package Impersonation
item.description: Most malicious open source packages now mimic real code rather than rely on typosquatting

item.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver a credential-stealing malware family dubbed EKZ Infostealer.
"The campaign abused trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deliver malware across managed endpoints," Arctic Wolf said. "Threat actors disguised the credential stealer
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google’s $135M Android Privacy Settlement: Who May Be Eligible
item.description: <p>Google’s $135 million Android settlement could pay eligible US users who used Android devices with cellular data since November 2017.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-privacy-settlement/">Google’s $135M Android Privacy Settlement: Who May Be Eligible</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
item.description:
Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better understand the impact and address them before they are publicly disclosed.
The development comes after a researcher named Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) disclosed details of multiple zero-day
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
item.description: Every time you think the industry has finally stopped doing some reckless, low-effort crap, somebody spins up a fresh box full of sketchy loaders, fake installers, recycled social-engineering bait, and enough exposed infrastructure to make you wonder if prod is just a public beta now - meanwhile some researcher casually drops a technique that turns a "minor" foothold into total account
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
item.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: Fahmida Y. Rashid, Kristina Beek

item.title: Microsoft Condemns "Uncoordinated" Zero Day Disclosures
item.description: Microsoft warned the disclosure of several unpatched vulnerabilities without notice has put “customers at unnecessary risk”

item.title: New AI Usage Report: Enterprise AI Risk Is Heavily Concentrated Among a Small Group of AI "Power users"
item.description: State of AI Usage Report 2026 (full report here) by LayerX Security reveals the extent of the enterprise AI visibility gap and why most organizations still don't understand where their AI exposure is actually coming from. The research shows that enterprise AI risk is not distributed evenly across users or platforms. Instead, it is heavily concentrated among a small group of AI power users and a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Threat Actor Jinx-0164 Targets Crypto Developers on macOS
item.description: New actor Jinx-0164 hit crypto developers with fake recruiter lures and macOS malware

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Cybersecurity Staff Prefer CISOs With Real Attack Response Experience, Study Reveals
item.description: ISC2 survey of cybersecurity professionals suggests that staff want their information security leaders to have experienced reacting to a significant cyber incident

item.title: GCHQ Chief Urges Action as AI Reshapes Cyber Threats
item.description: GCHQ director urges urgent business cyber action as AI and quantum reshape the threat

item.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
item.description: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: ESET APT Activity Report Q4 2025–Q1 2026
item.description: An overview of the activities of selected APT groups investigated and analyzed by ESET Research in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026

item.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
item.description:
A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social engineering and bespoke macOS malware.
"These campaigns leveraged sophisticated social engineering techniques, custom macOS malware, and deep targeting of CI/CD infrastructure," Wiz researchers Shira Ayal,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
item.description: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
item.description: The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and social-engineering its way into servers and databases.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
item.description: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
item.description: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Grandoreiro Malware and BTMOB RAT Campaigns Target Windows and Android Users
item.description:
Latin America and Europe become the target of two banking trojan campaigns that are designed to infect Windows and Android devices with Grandoreiro and BTMOB malware, respectively.
That's according to new findings from WatchGuard and ESET, which have observed the two malware families being used to single out companies in Spain, Portugal, and Mexico, as well as mobile users in Brazil.
The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious npm Package Stole Files From Claude AI User Directory via GitHub
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malicious package on the npm registry that comes with information stealing capabilities.
According to OX Security, the package, named "mouse5212-super-formatter," is designed to upload files from "/mnt/user-data," a dedicated directory used by Anthropic's Claude artificial intelligence (AI) tool to handle uploads and outputs in the background. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 7-Eleven Breach: Hackers Claim 600,000 Records Stolen
item.description: <p>7-Eleven says a system intrusion exposed franchise applicant data, with reports linking the breach to ShinyHunters and 185,000 affected people.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-7-eleven-data-breach-franchise-applicants/">7-Eleven Breach: Hackers Claim 600,000 Records Stolen</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: FBI’s 2025 Internet Crime Report
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: CrowdStrike, Google Take Down Glassworm Botnet
item.description: Operators of the malicious Glassworm botnet have been targeting software developers since at least early 2025

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Why Burnout in Cybersecurity Demands Risk-Based Response
item.description: Cybermindz warns that cybersecurity burnout is a growing risk, urging organizations to move beyond wellness initiatives and adopt a measurable, risk-based approach to workforce stress

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description:
When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee should do: finding faster ways to work.
Across most organizations today, employees are running three to five AI tools on any given day. Most were never reviewed by IT. A significant portion connects
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: All Major LLMs Exposed to Multi-Turn Manipulation, Warn Researchers
Cybersecurity Evolution: How We Went From Perimeter Defense to AI-Native Security
item.description: The cybersecurity industry of 2006 barely resembled today's billion-dollar behemoth. As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary celebration, we trace the industry's evolution through a technology lens.
item.dc:creator: Fahmida Y. Rashid

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown Disrupts Developer Supply Chain Attack Infrastructure
item.description:
CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent software chain campaign targeting software developers through malicious packages and extensions.
"Since at least early 2025, GlassWorm operators have systematically targeted software developers, a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 3 SOC Steps that Shut Down Incident Risks Early
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Thousands of Fake FIFA Domains Target World Cup Fans
item.description: Group-IB uncovered Ghost Stadium phishing and 4300 fake FIFA World Cup domains targeting fans
